generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T LACTIC ACID. Lactic acid, C 3 H 6 O 3 . Preparation for use , Dilutions. Authorities. 1 , Dr. S. Swan, proving with the 15th dil., obtained from Dr. Reisig ( Note: The preparation was doubtless, sour milk, and not pure lactic acid), drop doses every hour for ten hours, N. Am. J. of Hom., 1871, p. 569; 2 , same, effects of 30th, in a lady-patient, reported by Dr. B. Fincke; 3 , same, effects of 30th, in Dr. B. Fincke ( Nos . 4 to 18 , provings by T. F. Allen, with pure acid, N. Y. J. of Hom ., 1, p. 337); 4 , J. H. Buffum, two doses of 30th dil.; 5 , W. H. T., 30th dil.; 6 , F. R., 30th dil.; 7 , R. S. M., 30th dil.; 8 , C. A. S., 30th dil.; 9 , J. P. W., 30th dil.; 10 , J. R. P., 30th dil.; 11 , B. W., 30th dil.; 12 , A. B. C., 30th dil.; 13 , D. A. Babcock, 30th dil.; 14 , Dr. Simmons, Jr., 30th dil.; 15 , A. W. J., two provings, each, with one dose of 30th dil.; 16 , F. R., several doses of 1st dil.; 17 , Blanck, several doses of 1st dil.; 17 a , same, second proving; 18 , Dr. S., one dose of the 1/10th; 19 , Miss Sarah Tremper, proving with 200th dil., drop dose every hour till effects appeared, from MS. by Dr. Swan., N. Y. J. of Hom., 1, 337; 19 a , same, proving with several doses of 1m; 19 b , same, several doses of 10m; 20 , a man suffering from diabetes took 15 drops four times a day; doses doubled next day; after three days, repeated the experiment; from Foster's Clinical Med., 1874, "the production of acute rheumatism by lactic acid;" 20 a , same, effect of 75 drops in course of twenty-four hours; 20 b , same, 30 drops in one forenoon; 21 , effects in another case of diabetes; from Foster. MIND. ► Exalted condition of the brain and special sense; memory improved (third day), [7] . ► Mind clear; everything seems very distinct (morning of second and fourth days), [9] . ► Almost impossible to write correctly; drops out words and misspells (second day), [4] . ► Memory seems affected; cannot remember anything an hour after it has happened; unusual (fifth day), [19a] . HEAD. ► Vertigo. ► Vertigo at 8 P.M. (second day), [10] . ► Vertigo when turning the head; suddenly; fulness of the head (second day), [1] . ► Vertigo, with heat in the head (third day), [10] . ► Vertigo and nausea on rising (third day), [10] . ► Vertigo, and sleepless at night (first day), [19a] . ► General Head. [10.] ► Rush of blood quickly to the head on rising from a seat at 11 A.M.; everything turned black before the eyes, and she nearly fell down (sixteenth day), [19b] . ► Congestion of the head (thirteenth and fourteenth days), [19b] . ► Congestion of the head, painless, with strong pulsation of the vessels of the neck (5 P.M., third day), [7] . ► Sensation as if all the blood was trickling down from the front to the back of the head (tenth day), [19] . ► Lightness of head (twelfth day), [19] . ► Head dull all day, very nervous about it (seventh day), [19b] . ► Head dull and heavy, with occasional pain in the forehead and eyes (second day), [19b] . ► Great pain in the head, worse on the left side, at 7 P.M. (second day), [10] . ► Heavy, dull pain in the head at 8 P.M. (second day), [10] . ► Fulness in the head, it feels too heavy, at 8 P.M. (third hour), [10] . [20.] ► Fulness, as if the top of the head would rise off (fourth hour), [10] . ► Fulness of the head all day; on stooping, it seemed as if the blood would spurt from the nose; relieved at 10 P.M. (twelfth day), [19] . ► Headache at the base of the brain, extending up and over to the vertex; relieved by pressing the head behind the orbits; with flushed face and rheumatic pains in the bones; aggravated by motion (fourth day), [7] . ► Head began to ache at 11 A.M.; increased till 3 P.M., when it seemed as if it would burst (fifth day), [19a] . ► Slight headache during the evening; a dull pain on both sides of the forehead, with a sensation of fulness (second day), [14] . ► Forehead. ► Slight pain in the forehead, and head is sore to the touch (fourth day), [19] . ► Slight pain in the forehead just over the eyes; the pain increased during the day, and at 8 P.M. it was felt all over the head and eyes; the rush of blood to the head and face caused a distressing fulness (ninth day), [19b] . ► Sensation of fulness in the forehead, as if the brain were too large, during the day (second day), [16] . ► Congestion, as if the blood would burst out of the forehead and eyes; and at 11 A.M., while sitting in church, felt as if the blood would spurt from the nose, and as if it were impossible to sit another minute (seventh day), [19b] . ► Dull pain in the forehead, with desire to close the eyes (second day), [1] . [30.] ► Dull, heavy pain across the forehead, extends into the left eye, as if behind the pupil (third day), [19a] . ► Pain in the forehead above the eyes; increased and became very severe at noon; head dull from 12 to 3 P.M., less severe after 1 P.M.; pain in the head grew worse, and seemed to go all through the head and eyes (fourth day), [19a] . ► Pain in the forehead just over the eyes, dull and heavy, from 12 to 4; it is sharp and severe, and extends into the left eye; ceased at 4 P.M. (first and second days), [19b] . ► Sharp pain across forehead, painful to touch, with nausea (eighth day), [19b] . ► Sharp pain in the forehead just above the eyes; appeared in the evening, after the second dose, and lasted all night, [19a] . ► Headache in the forehead on waking, worse at 8 A.M.; continues at intervals all day (sixth day), [19] . ► Headache across the forehead, worse on the left side (sixth day), [19] . ► Headache in forehead over the eyes at 10 A.M. (tenth day), [19] . ► Slight headache across the forehead and eyes at various times during the day (first day), [19b] . ► Dull headache across forehead and eyes on waking, worse in the left eye (third, fourth, and fifth days), [19b] . ► Temples. [40.] ► Pain extending from the ear to the temples and the top of the head (fourth hour), [10] . ► Throbbing in the temples, evening (second day), [10] . ► Vertex. ► Full feeling across the top of the head at 10 A.M. increasing till 11 A.M., as if all the blood in the body were in the head and face; at 12.25 P.M. it was very severe, and extended into the eyes, worse on the left side; ceased at 10 P.M. (second day), [19] . ► Top of the head feels as if it would burst at 10 A.M. (fifth day), [19] . ► Sharp pain across the top of the head; it is sore to touch; the soreness did not continue long, but the head ached from 1 till 5 P.M. (fifth day), [19] . ► Parietals. ► Pain in the left side of the head, and a lifting, as if the top were rising off (third day), [10] . ► Pain extending from the temple to the occiput, on the left side, on rising in the morning, with a feeling of weakness; soreness of the scalp; it smarts and burns (third day), [11] . ► Occiput. ► Woke at 2 A.M., with pain at the base of the occiput and over the eyes; very restless till rising at 6 A.M.; mind clear, everything seemed very distinct (eighth hour), [9] . ► Slight pain in the back of the head, extending from the occiput to the top of the head, with roughness and constriction in the throat (sixth hour), [9] . ► Dull, weighty pain in the occiput (first hour); on lying down for bed, the pain left the occiput and seated itself in the forehead, so severe as to keep him awake nearly two hours; on waking in the morning, the same pain in the forehead, with a tendency to close the eyes; at 2 P.M., the pain changed back to the occiput, the eyes still heavy; this continued in the occiput till lying down for bed, when it again changed to the forehead; next morning, well; on repeating the drug at 5 P.M. the pain returned in the occiput in two hours, and on lying down again, changed to the forehead; next morning, well; this was subsequently twice repeated, [13] . [50.] ► Severe headache in the left mastoid bone, extending obliquely across, by way of the occiput, to over the right ear (fourth day), [7] . ► Occasional slight darting pains, which start from the centre of the brain and move backward in straight lines to the centre of the occipital protuberance, aggravated by motion (first day, first and second provings), [15] . EYE. ► Objective. ► Marked protrusion of the eyes, with dilated pupils (fourth day), [1] . ► Subjective. ► Dull, heavy feeling in eyes, with tendency to close them, with the headache, [13] . ► Pain in the left eye and brow, extending across the forehead, continuing from 2 P.M., the rest of the day (fourth day), [19] . ► Pain in the eyes, worse on closing them, but nearly as severe on opening them (fourth day), [19a] . ► Sensation of fulness in the eyes, and a confused, bursting-out feeling on looking steadily at any object, especially a bright light; relieved by closing the lids; pupils dilated (first day), [15] . ► Eyes feel as if they would burst open or burst out of the head, worse on turning the head; lasted, very severe, all night (fifth day), [19a] . ► Congestion, as if the blood were forcing its way out of the eyes (second day), [19b] . ► Sharp pain in left eye, more while reading, with photophobia (fifth day), [19] . ► Lids. [60.] ► Peculiar twitching across the right upper lid, about 10 A.M., transient; in the left eye at 11 A.M. (second day), [19] . ► Jerking of the left upper lid (third day), [11] . ► Ball. ► Pressure on the cornea is very painful (fourth day), [19a] . ► Pupil. ► Pupils somewhat enlarged, with slight pain in the balls (fourth day), [5] . ► Pupils dilated (fourth day), [7] ; (first day), [15] , [10] . ► Pupils much dilated, attract attention, 6 P.M. (fifth day), [13] . ► Pupils dilated more afternoons and mornings, [15] . ► Vision. ► Smoky appearance of the room (second day), [10] . ► Dark clouds pass before the eyes, cannot see the letters on the blackboard (second day), [10] . ► Feels for another step when at the bottom of the stairs (second day), [10] . EAR. [70.] ► Cold spot over the right ear, it feels like a stone, evening (second day), [10] . ► Pain extends into the ear from the parotid glands, [10] . ► Hearing. ► Singing and snapping in left ear (second day), [10] . ► Roaring in left ear on rising and in afternoon (third day), [10] . NOSE. ► Objective. ► A sore, like a cold-sore, inside the nostrils (fourth day), [9] . ► Discharge (from nose) white and frothy, a little thicker than water (thirteenth day), [19] . ► Catarrhus nasi, with copious thick, yellow mucous discharge, [2] . ► Nose bled a little at 8 P.M. (fourteenth day), [19b] . ► Bloody discharge from nose several times (seventeenth day), [19] . ► Subjective. ► Nose stopped during morning (twelfth day), [19] . [80.] ► Nose very dry and burning (second day), [4] . ► Feeling as if blood would spurt from the nose (twelfth day), [19] . FACE. ► Face flushed with the headache (fourth day), [7] . ► Rush of blood to face and head (ninth day), [19b] . ► Pain extends from the left eye to the eye-tooth, like neuralgia (fourteenth day), [19a] . MOUTH. ► Tongue. ► Tongue coated thick white (first to sixth day), [19] . ► Tongue coated thick yellow (fourteenth day), [19] , [19a] ; (third day), [19b] . ► Tongue bright red on the edges, with a sore, raw sensation on the right side (fifth day), [5] . ► Tongue feels large is coated white (third day), [15a] . ► Tongue sore on the left side, raw, and red, [17a] . ► General Mouth. [90.] ► Mouth dry (third day), [15a] . ► Mouth dry and tastes like copper (third day), [15] . ► Roof of mouth dry; cannot keep the tongue from constantly moving over the roof of the mouth; sensibility increased; felt burnt (first hour), [4] . ► Mouth and fauces feel dry; constant inclination to swallow; not relieved by drinking (first hour), [4] . ► Great dryness in the mouth and throat (first hour), [19] . ► Mouth hot; the tongue seems to fill it up (fourth day), [15a] . ► Saliva. ► Commenced soon to secrete large quantities of saliva, which in the morning was found to contain grayish lumps, afterwards the expectoration was yellowish, and tasted salty; this appeared to be secreted just inside the glottis (sixth day), [1] . ► Mouth constantly full of water, worse after each eructation, [18] . ► Saliva salt, like brine, continued several days, [18] . ► Taste. ► Bad taste in the mouth (fifth day), [19] ; (sixth day), [19a] . [100.] ► Taste fetid (third day), [10] . ► Peculiar taste on the tongue posteriorly, extending into the throat, with immediate accumulation of saliva, [3] . ► Taste acrid, with nausea, like copper (first hour), [10] . ► Taste metallic (first day), [15a] . ► Sour, metallic taste, lasts fifteen minutes after a dose, repeatedly, [14] . ► Taste like copper (third day), [15] . ► Taste like Lobelia or tobacco (first hour), [10] . THROAT. ► Sense of a plug in the throat, with scraping and dryness a feeling like that from tobacco (second hour), [10] . ► Fulness in the throat, which keeps him swallowing frothy mucus; the lump or fulness feels like a small puff-ball; not relieved by swallowing (morning of fifth day); this returns in the evening after another dose, and is repeated after subsequent doses, [14] . ► Constriction and soreness of the throat, with difficulty of swallowing solids, at 5 P.M. (third day), [7] . [110.] ► Throat constricted and dry ("similar to Lachesis"), (second day), [9] . ► Throat constricted and rough, with pain in occiput, etc. (sixth hour), [9] . ► Constricted feeling low down in the throat, with slight nausea, causing a severe paroxysm of coughing (first hour), [8] . ► Neck feels too short by two inches (second and third days), [1] . ► Uvula. ► Elongation of uvula (fifth day), [1] . ► Fauces, Pharynx, and sophagus. ► Mucous membrane of fauces dry, red, and congested (third hour), [4] . ► Dryness of posterior fauces, with hawking of mucus from posterior nares (second day), [1] . ► Intense burning in the sophagus after an eructation, with a qualmish feeling, and an increase of saliva, [18] . ► Fulness of the fauces, with intense burning in the throat, fauces, and sophagus, [18] . ► Constriction in the fauces, with dull pressure; acrid taste, with nausea; mouth tastes like copper (first hour), [10] . [120.] ► Rawness of pharynx from posterior nares, left side, down to sophagus, with sensation of a quantity of mucus lodged in the nares and larynx, causing hawking; hawking does not seem to relieve it entirely, a portion only of clear viscid mucus coming away (third and fourth days), [1] . ► Swallowing. ► Swallowing solid food very difficult; it is almost impossible to force solid food into the sophagus, semifluids more easily, and fluids easily swallowed (evening, second day), [12] . ► External Throat. ► Pain in the submaxillary and parotid glands, a stiff feeling in the parotids, and a pain extending from them into the ear, at 8 P.M. (third hour), [10] . ► Strong pulsation of the vessels of the neck, with painless congestion of the head, at 5 P.M. (third day), [7] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Appetite not very good, and apparent indifference to food; very little satisfies; what is eaten seems to sour on the stomach, with eructations and a great deal of flatus (fourth day), [9] . ► No appetite for supper (second day), [10] . ► No desire for breakfast; the stomach seems to come almost up into the mouth; worse on sitting still (fourth day), [15] . ► Thirst. ► Thirst but slightly relieved by drinking, in the evening (second day), [12] . ► Eructation. ► Constant desire to eructate, which he dreads on account of the burning; worse from smoking, [18] . ► *Eructations of hot, acrid fluid, which burns from the stomach to the throat , [18] . [130.] ► *Eructations of burning hot gas from the stomach, causing a profuse secretion of tenacious mucus, which must be constantly hawked up ; aggravated by smoking tobacco, [18] . ► Belching, with taste of ingesta (third and fourth day), [1] . ► Nausea. ► *Nausea (third day), [9] . ► Nausea all the morning, and till 3 P.M. (first day), [19] . ► Nausea on awaking, with a sensation of weight low down in the stomach, with pains in the head darting from the centre of the brain in straight likes to the occiput; pains predominate in the back of the head (second day), [15] . ► Nausea continues till 10 A.M. (fourth day), [15] . ► Woke suddenly at 5.30 A.M., with very much nausea, which increased till he vomits a thin, colorless, watery stuff; somewhat relieved after getting out of bed (fourth day), [15a] . ► Nausea, especially at 6 A.M., before rising; a faint, gone feeling low down in the stomach (first day), [15a] . ► Nausea on rising in the morning, with vertigo (third day), [10] . ► Nausea on rising in the morning; after eating, sick feeling, not amounting to nausea, with taste of food (third and fourth day), [1] . [140.] ► Nausea shortly after rising, lasts till 10 A.M.; it returned from 3 to 5 P.M., and again in the evening half an hour after eating (fifth day), [17] . ► Nausea, shortly after rising; relieved by breakfast; returned and lasted till 10 A.M. (sixth day), [17] . ► *Nausea came on half an hour after breakfast, not severe enough to cause vomiting, but every persistent ; relief for an hour in the afternoon, but returned at 2 P.M., and continued till he fell asleep, at midnight (third day), [17] . ► Nausea at 10.30 A.M. (third day), [19a] . ► Nausea continues till 11 A.M., when it gradually wears away (fourth day), [15a] . ► Nausea during afternoon and evening (first day), [19b] . ► Nausea all the afternoon, but could not vomit (fourth day), [19a] . ► Nausea, with retching and waterbrash, after eating (second day), [1] . ► Nausea; unable to eat the usual breakfast, food seems loathsome; sickness, almost to vomiting; relieved after eating (third day), [15] . ► Nausea relieved by eating breakfast (third day), [19b] . [150.] ► Nausea, stinging pain in the stomach, whirling around at the pit of the stomach, with feeling of pressure (third day), [10] . ► Nausea and faint feeling, with a paroxysm of coughing as before (half an hour after another dose), [8] . ► *Constant nausea all through the proving, [17a] . ► Constant nausea after 9 A.M., not affected by eating; appetite good (second day), [17] . ► Slight nausea, some flatus, and pain in the umbilical region (fourth hour), [9] . ► Slight nausea and a constricted feeling low down in the throat, causing a severe paroxysm of coughing (first hour), [8] . ► Nausea continues, and is aggravated by motion, especially on getting up in the morning, at 8 A.M. (second day), [8] . ► Stomach. ► Faintness in the stomach, lasted about an hour, then gradually passed off, leaving nausea, at 2 P.M., one half-hour after a dose (second day), [8] . ► Heat in the stomach; gradually disappeared in a few minutes, [12] . ► Burning and weight in the stomach, aggravated by smoking, [16] . [160.] ► Fulness and heat at the pit of the stomach, with nausea and pain around the umbilicus (second day), [10] . ABDOMEN. ► Umbilical and Sides. ► Pain around the umbilicus (second day), [10] . ► Stinging pain in left side of abdomen, running into the spermatic cord, in the morning (third day), [10] . ► General Abdomen. ► Bearing-down pain in the upper part of the abdomen (second day), [19a] . ► Jerking and sharp cutting pain in the muscles of the abdomen; waked from sleep, with a scream, at midnight (second day), [10] . ► Stinging pain in the muscles of the abdomen, at 4 P.M. (second day), [10] . ► Hypogastrium. ► Pain across lower part of bowels on the right side (fourth day), [19] . ► Colic in the lower part of the abdomen, which nothing seemed to relieve; stool normal (second day), [4] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Protrusion of piles after a stool, very painful; had to sit down two or three times; could not walk (thirteenth day), [19b] . ► Severe aching pain in the anus, waking out of sleep; relieved by stool, at 2.30 A.M. (fourth day), [5] . [170.] ► Tenesmus (third day), [10] . STOOL. ► Diarrha preceded by sharp pain across the lower abdomen (second day), [19a] . ► Diarrhic stool, hurrying to the closet, without pain in the rectum, [17] . ► Loose bowels, with transient pain in the abdomen after the stool (fifth day), [19] . ► Soft stool, with hard lumps, in the evening (second day), [10] . ► Soft, mushy stool at 9 A.M., again at 2 P.M. and 6 P.M.; had hardly time to get to the closet (second day), [5] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder. ► Bladder feels sore, as though it had been overtaxed or strained; must keep bent forward to take off the pressure, in which position he feels much more comfortable (third day), [14] . ► Micturition. ► Desire to urinate oftener than usual, but did not pass much at a time; urine high-colored, [17] , [17a] . ► Frequent desire to urinate (tenth day), [19] . ► Urine passed frequently and in large quantities (repeated through two provings), [14] . [180.] ► *Urinates frequently, day and night; the attempt to retain it causes pain , [14] . ► Called out of bed at 3 A.M. to urinate; urine profuse and pale; urinated again at 8 A.M.; copious, light lemon-color (sixth day), [13] . ► Rises four times in one night to urinate; urine light straw-color (third day), [14] . ► Seemed to be fast turning to urine, and running away through his penis; had to stop proving; a renewal of the proving brings it on again, with the sore stretched feeling in the bladder, [14] . ► Before proving, troubled with a desire to urinate every half hour; since taking the drug, has urinated only four or five times in twenty-four hours, [11] . ► Urine. ► Urine white and thick, in the morning (second day), [19b] . ► Urine high-colored, but clear (tenth day), [19] . ► Urine light-reddish, but clear (second day), [19] ; (third day), [19b] . ► Urine somewhat increased; passed with difficulty (fourth day), [9] . ► Urine scanty and high-colored (second and third day), [10] . [190.] ► Urine normal in color and quantity, but has a greasy pellicle on the surface (first day), [4] . ► Urine cloudy and dark (first day), [4] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Menses more profuse than usual; two days too early, [19a] . ► Menses scanty, seventeen days too late; in the morning had pain across the small of the back, and in the lower part of the abdomen; menses appeared at 11 A.M.; with a very distressed feeling across the lower part of the abdomen; back pained all day and night; flow scanty and pale, [19] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Larynx. ► Dryness and burning of larynx (after twelve hours, second day), [1] . ► Dryness and rawness, extending to larynx, the rim of the glottis being particularly affected (fifth day), [1] . ► Rawness and swelling increased, with spasm of the glottis, awaking me from sleep and causing severe pain and suffocation (fourth night), [1] . ► Voice. ► For an hour and a half after rising, my voice was entirely without control , whispering and speaking when expecting to speak loud; croaking and deep voice; this continued more or less through the day (sixth day); and with the other symptoms gradually disappeared the day after, [1] . ► Some hoarseness; sensitive to the cold air; (here followed a cold, for which other remedies were taken), (second day), [9] . ► Cough and Expectoration. ► Severe paroxysm of coughing, with slight nausea, caused by a constricted feeling low down in the throat (first hour), [8] . [200.] ► Spasmodic, ringing cough, caused by irritation of the throat from hot eructations, aggravated by smoking tobacco, [18] . ► Dry cough all day (twelfth to fourteenth day), [19] . ► Secretion of and a hemming up of small lumps of tough mucus of grayish color (fifth day), [1] . ► Respiration. ► Sawlike, rasping sound of respiration (fifth day), (took Bell. 1m), [1] . CHEST. ► Left lung and back of the breast sore all day, but did not pain (fifth day), [19] . ► Pain in the lower part of the left side, extended around to the back, soon passed off, after eating, at 6 P.M. (fourth day), [19a] . ► Woke at midnight with a scream, and pain and cramp in the muscles of the left side, with jerking and sharp cutting pain in the muscles of the abdomen (second day), [10] . ► Sharp transient pain in the lower part of the left side, at 10.30 P.M. (second day), [19] . ► At 9 A.M., a severe, sharp, cutting or sticking pain in the upper third of the right side; worse from motion; most relief from folding the arms across the chest; it becomes so severe that he is obliged to take Bryonia, which relieves him, but the side remains very sore; after another dose of Lactic acid, the pain and soreness returned; is compelled to keep the arm close to the side to avoid pain; it is very difficult to put his coat on or off; repeated in subsequent proving, [14] . ► Pain in the back part of right breast at intervals during the morning, till 11 A.M., when the pain extended to the axilla, which feels sore to the touch (fourth day), [19] . [210.] ► Pain in the back of the left breast, two or three times during the day (sixth day), [19] . ► Sharp pain in right breast at 11 A.M. (sixth day), [19] . ► Sharp pain darted through the lower part of the right breast at 1 P.M., and continued at intervals during the afternoon (fourth day), [19] . ► Sharp pain in back part of the left breast during the afternoon; at 6 P.M., a similar pain in the right breast, after which is felt sore to the touch; soon passed away (second day), [19] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Præcordium. ► The heart-sounds were clear (sixth day), [20a] . ► Slight depression and constriction in the cardiac region; relieved by bending the chest forward; in the forenoon (second day) and repeated, [12] . ► Sharp pain around the heart, causing palpitation, a few minutes after 11.30 A.M.; lasts till 2 P.M. (second day), [19] . ► Pulse. ► The pulse rose 12 beats (after three days), [20a] . ► The pulse was 102 (sixth day), [20a] . ► Pulse 98, full and soft, [20b] . [220.] ► Pulse 96, full and soft, [21] . ► Pulse 90 and full (third day), [20] . ► Pulse 90, feverish (third day), [15] . ► Pulse 62, small and feeble (second day, half an hour after a dose), [10] . NECK AND BACK. ► Rheumatic pain in the right side of the neck and right breast, worse from stooping the head; continued about an hour (sixth day), [18] . ► Great distress in the region of the kidneys (tenth day), [19] . ► Pain in the lower part of the back and head all night; the pain extended to the shoulders, especially the right, [19] . ► Pain in the small of the back at 11 A.M.; extends up the left shoulder and side of the neck; did not last long in the neck and shoulder, but continued in the back all day (second day), [19] . ► Pain in the small of the back about noon; continued all night (fourth and fifth day), [19b] . ► Pain in the small of the back all night, could hardly lie in bed (first and second day); extends to the shoulders (third day), [19a] . [230.] ► Pain in the region of the kidneys, from 11 to 12 A.M. (fourth day), [19] . ► On retiring, a severe pain in the left lumbar region, more a weak feeling, which is relieved by turning on to the side (first and second day), [4] . ► Aching in the lower part of the back, from 3 to 10 P.M. (first day), [19] . ► Sharp pain across the small of the back and shoulders, worse when walking (fifth day), [19] . ► Sore pain across the lower part of the back all day (second day), [19b] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► The attempt to take the acid was always followed by pains in the joints, [21] . ► Pains in the joints, in the evening (second day), [30] . ► Severe pain in various joints, [21] . ► Acute pains in the joints and flying pains about the limbs, [20] . ► After taking it for a week in May, he was laid up with such severe joint pains that I was called to visit him and found him in bed with pains in his elbows, shoulders, ankles, knees, and, as he said, all over him, [21] . [240.] ► He had a bad night from pains in the joints, which disturbed him very much, and which came on suddenly after midnight (fifth night); the next morning the metacarpo-phalangeal and first phalangeal articulations of the first and second fingers of each hand were found to be red, swollen, hot, and painful; the slightest movement aggravated the pain, and he could not on this account pick up anything with his fingers; the acid mixture was stopped, and in the evening the pain in the knuckles was less, and the redness was diminished; they were, however, still stiff; no other joints were affected (sixth day), [20a] . ► Elbows and knees painful and stiff, [20b] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Shoulder. ► Pain in the shoulder on moving the arm, with soreness in the side (third day), [14] . ► Rheumatic pains in both shoulders, running up the muscles of the neck to the mastoid process, and into the ears, [17a] . ► Wrist. ► Slight rheumatic pains in the wrist-joint (half an hour), [8] . ► Flying pains about the joints for four days, after which he was disturbed at night be severe pain in the right wrist, which in the morning was red, swollen, painful, and hot, and was a typical specimen of a rheumatic joints, [20b] . ► Hand. ► Small joints of both hands, wrists, and in the last degree the elbows are red, swollen, and painful; they seem typical symptoms of acute rheumatic arthritis; in the evening both wrists, the small joints of the fingers and the elbows are all red, hot, swollen, tender, and painful (third day). Lactic acid was discontinued, the next day the joints were better, [20] . ► Pains returned in the knuckles in the evening; they were swollen, red, and tender, [20b] . ► Fingers. ► Pain in the second joint of the right thumb, at 1 P.M., for an hour (second day), [19] . ► Index finger, right, swollen, two bright red spots on it, one between first and second, the other between second and third joints; the last itched once or twice during the day; from 2 to 10 P.M., it itched and burned like a bee-sting; on the fifth day it was sore between the first and second joints, but not red; between the second and third joints red, but not sore; the red spot looked like a blister, [19] .
